text: Code reviews are an important practice in software development. When team members review each other's code, they can catch bugs early, before the code goes into production. This saves time and effort because fixing problems later is much harder. Besides finding errors, code reviews also help with knowledge sharing. A junior developer might learn a better way to write code, while a senior can learn about new features or approaches. Over time, this process improves the overall quality of both the code and the team. Everyone becomes more familiar with the codebase and works together more effectively.
